data.title: Uncertainty in Today's World Amid Government Actions and AI Advancements
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. In today's rapidly evolving world, uncertainty prevails as individuals grapple with distinguishing reality from fiction. This ambiguity stems from both unpredictable government actions and the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI). The complexity of these developments leaves many questioning the authenticity of information and events. As AI continues to evolve, its impact on society and governance remains a topic of significant discussion and concern.

data.title: New Zealand's Migration Decline Hits Decade Low
data.description: New Zealand's net migration has reached its lowest point in over ten years, excluding the pandemic period, as the number of arrivals decreased and more New Zealanders moved to Australia in search of better opportunities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the trend of New Zealanders relocating to Australia, driven by the pursuit of improved prospects. The decline in net migration reflects a significant shift in movement patterns, with fewer people entering New Zealand and an increasing number of citizens opting to leave. This trend has implications for the country's labor market and economic dynamics, as the flow of people impacts various sectors. The migration figures underscore the challenges New Zealand faces in retaining its workforce and attracting new talent amid global competition for skilled workers.

data.title: Asian Currencies Stabilize Amid Risk-Off Sentiment
data.description: Asian currencies are showing signs of stabilization as market participants remain cautious due to prevailing risk-off sentiment. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that investors are closely monitoring global economic indicators and geopolitical developments, which could influence currency movements in the region. Analysts suggest that the current market environment, characterized by uncertainty and volatility, may continue to impact the performance of Asian currencies. Traders are advised to stay vigilant as external factors, including central bank policies and international trade dynamics, play a significant role in shaping the currency landscape.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 66,000 USDT with a 2.28% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 13, 2026, 00:34 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 66,000 USDT and is now trading at 65,924.609375 USDT, with a narrowed 2.28% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Federal Reserve Identified as Major Risk to U.S. Economic Growth
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan has identified the Federal Reserve as one of the biggest risks to U.S. economic growth. According to ChainCatcher, this statement highlights concerns about the central bank's influence on the economy. The remarks underscore ongoing debates about the Federal Reserve's role in shaping economic policy and its potential impact on future growth.

data.title: Pump.fun mobile app launches GitHub creator fee sharing feature
data.description: Pump.fun announced on the X platform that the GitHub creator fee sharing feature is now live. Users can now distribute creator fees to any GitHub account via the Pump.fun mobile app. More social media integration features are coming soon.

data.title: Japanese Authorities Detain Chinese Fishing Vessel in Exclusive Economic Zone
data.description: Japan's Fisheries Agency has detained a Chinese fishing vessel that entered Japan's exclusive economic zone near Nagasaki prefecture. Bloomberg posted on X that the vessel was seized after it did not comply with a request for inspection and attempted to flee. The incident highlights ongoing tensions in maritime activities in the region.

data.title: Canada's Electric Vehicle Deal with China May Impact U.S. Trade Talks
data.description: The Canadian government's agreement to permit Chinese-manufactured electric vehicles into the country is reportedly jeopardizing crucial trade discussions with the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns raised by a representative for American automakers. The deal has sparked apprehension among U.S. industry leaders who fear it could undermine ongoing negotiations between Canada and the United States. The representative emphasized the potential negative impact on trade relations, urging Canadian officials to reconsider the agreement. The situation underscores the complexities of international trade dynamics and the challenges faced by countries in balancing economic interests.

data.title: Coinbase 2025 Financial Report: Q4 pressured with a net loss of $667 million, but full-year performance remains strong.
data.description: Coinbase released its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results. Despite the overall downturn in the crypto market, Coinbase achieved several all-time highs, with trading volume and market share doubling. However, Q4 revenue slightly missed expectations and a net loss was recorded. Coinbase recorded a net loss of $667 million, or $2.49 per share, in Q4, far exceeding analysts' expectations. Total revenue was $1.78 billion, a 5% decrease sequentially and approximately a 22% decrease year-over-year, falling short of market expectations of $1.83 billion to $1.85 billion. Adjusted earnings per share were $0.66, adjusted net income was $178 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $566 million. Coinbase faced pressure in the fourth quarter but maintained a strong full-year performance, with total trading volume reaching $5.2 trillion, a 156% year-over-year increase. Its market share in the crypto trading arena doubled to approximately 6.4%, and subscription and service revenue grew by 23% to approximately $2.8 billion. Coinbase One's paid subscribers approached 1 million, and platform assets and USDC balances reached all-time highs. Coinbase stated it is advancing its "Everything Exchange" strategy, including derivatives expansion and stablecoin payments. Despite short-term drag from the bear market, Coinbase views 2025 as a strong year and remains optimistic about product innovation and market recovery in 2026.

data.title: Microsoft's AI CEO Predicts Automation of White-Collar Jobs Within 18 Months
data.description:
Microsoft's AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman has projected that artificial intelligence could fully automate most white-collar professional tasks within the next 12 to 18 months. According to NS3.AI, Suleyman emphasized that AI is approaching human-level performance in office-based roles. He also highlighted Microsoft's commitment to developing in-house AI technologies to ensure resilience.

The potential for AI to automate these tasks raises concerns about workforce displacement and economic impacts. Suleyman stressed the importance of developing controlled and safe AI systems to mitigate these challenges. The discussion underscores the need for careful management of AI advancements to balance technological progress with societal implications.

data.title: Blackstone Targets Japanese Millionaires in Wealth Expansion Strategy
data.description:
Blackstone is launching a significant media campaign to capture what it perceives as a major private wealth opportunity outside the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the firm's focus on the substantial amount of cash held by Japanese households, estimated at $7 trillion. This initiative aims to attract the attention of Japan's affluent individuals, who represent a significant portion of this wealth.

The strategy underscores Blackstone's ambition to expand its influence in the global financial market by tapping into the wealth reserves of Japanese millionaires. This move is part of a broader effort to diversify and grow its investment portfolio beyond the U.S. market.

Blackstone's approach involves leveraging media channels to effectively communicate its value proposition to potential investors in Japan. By doing so, the firm hopes to establish a strong foothold in the region and capitalize on the untapped financial resources available.

This campaign reflects a strategic shift in Blackstone's operations, as it seeks to engage with international markets and explore new avenues for growth. The focus on Japan's wealthy demographic is indicative of the firm's long-term vision to enhance its global presence and investment capabilities.

data.title: Peru Maintains Interest Rates Amid Inflation and Strong Sol
data.description: Peru's central bank has decided to keep its interest rates unchanged as inflation approaches the midpoint of its target range. Bloomberg posted on X that the Peruvian sol is currently near a six-year high, reflecting the country's economic stability. The decision to hold rates comes as the central bank monitors inflationary pressures and the currency's performance. This move aligns with the bank's strategy to balance economic growth with inflation control. The sol's strength is attributed to favorable economic conditions and investor confidence in Peru's financial policies. The central bank's decision underscores its commitment to maintaining economic stability while addressing inflation concerns.

data.title: Jim Bianco Discusses Software Stocks and Correlations
data.description: Jim Bianco, president of Bianco Research posted on X, discusses the end of correlations since April 30th, 2020, the end of the COVID recession. He questions if software stocks' recovery will affect this.

data.title: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio to Address Munich Security Conference
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio announced plans to deliver a speech titled 'A Changing World' at the Munich Security Conference on Saturday. According to Jin10, Rubio emphasized the significance of the conference, noting that attendees are eager for the United States to clearly outline its developmental trajectory and the nature of relationships it seeks to establish with allies.

data.title: Comparing Bank Runs: Banks, Casinos, and Exchanges
data.description: Crypto KOL Danny (@agintender) posted on X about the different approaches banks, casinos, and exchanges take towards bank runs. Banks operate on interest margins and often have insufficient cash reserves, making them vulnerable to bank runs. Casinos, on the other hand, rely on probability and typically maintain adequate cash reserves, so they are not as concerned about bank runs but are wary of accusations of unfairness. Exchanges, which earn through transaction fees and act as custodians of funds, are unlikely to lose money unless they engage in risky betting or misuse assets. They are more concerned about public perception and the withdrawal of liquidity.

data.title: Silicon Valley Billionaires Eye South Florida for Tax Benefits
data.description: South Florida is increasingly attracting Silicon Valley billionaires seeking a tax-friendly environment. Bloomberg posted on X that a proposed one-time wealth levy in California is enhancing the region's appeal. As California considers new tax measures, wealthy individuals are exploring relocation options to optimize their financial strategies. Miami, with its favorable tax policies, is emerging as a popular destination for those looking to minimize tax liabilities. The trend reflects a broader movement among affluent individuals to seek jurisdictions with more advantageous tax structures.

data.title: Grayscale CEO Addresses CFTC's Innovation Advisory Committee
data.description:
Grayscale CEO Peter Mintzberg recently addressed the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) Innovation Advisory Committee. Grayscale posted on X, highlighting the importance of regulatory clarity in the digital asset space. Mintzberg emphasized the need for collaboration between industry leaders and regulators to foster innovation while ensuring consumer protection.

During the meeting, Mintzberg discussed the evolving landscape of digital assets and the challenges faced by companies in navigating regulatory frameworks. He stressed the significance of establishing clear guidelines to support the growth and development of the sector.

The discussion also touched upon the role of technology in transforming financial services and the potential benefits of blockchain and cryptocurrency. Mintzberg advocated for a balanced approach that encourages innovation without compromising security and compliance.

Grayscale's participation in the committee underscores its commitment to advancing the digital asset industry through constructive dialogue with regulatory bodies. The company continues to engage with stakeholders to promote a sustainable and secure environment for digital asset investment.

data.title: Federal Reserve suggests new margin framework to address crypto volatility
data.description:
The Federal Reserve proposes treating crypto as its own asset class for margin requirements in derivatives trading.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/federal-reserve-suggests-margin-framework/

data.title: Oil Market Analysis and Updates
data.description:
The oil market is experiencing significant fluctuations, with various factors influencing prices and production levels. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions and economic indicators on the global oil supply. Analysts are closely monitoring developments in major oil-producing regions, including the Middle East and North America, as these areas play a crucial role in determining market trends.

Recent reports indicate that supply chain disruptions and changes in demand patterns are contributing to volatility in oil prices. Industry experts are assessing the potential effects of these disruptions on future production and pricing strategies. Additionally, environmental policies and technological advancements are shaping the long-term outlook for the oil industry.

Market participants are advised to stay informed about ongoing negotiations and policy changes that could affect oil production and distribution. As the situation evolves, stakeholders are encouraged to adapt their strategies to navigate the complexities of the current market environment.

data.title: Russia Considers Rejoining Dollar Settlement System Amid Global Market Developments
data.description: Russia is reportedly contemplating a return to the dollar settlement system, according to recent reports. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this potential move as part of broader global market shifts. Meanwhile, South Korea has speculated that Kim Jong-un's daughter may be designated as his successor. In China, authorities are set to impose strict penalties on false information lacking AI identification. These developments are part of ongoing changes in international markets, with implications for economic strategies worldwide.
